de.uka.ilkd.key.smt

Class ExternalProcessLauncher<T>

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.ExternalProcessLauncher<T>

  • public class ExternalProcessLauncher<T>
extends Object
    This class is responsible for starting external processes: 1. It creates the process 2. Creates a pipe, that is used for communication. 3. Starts the process and waits until the pipe has been closed or the process has been stopped. Remark: It blocks the invoking thread. The parameter T of the class can be used to define user-specific parameters.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private ReentrantLock lockProcess
      lock for the process-object in order to guarantee synchronous access: If you want to access on process then acquire first the lock!
      private Pipe<T> pipe 
      private Process process

      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      (package private) T getCommunication()
      Call this method only after the pipe has been stopped.
      void launch(String[] command, String initialMessage, PipeListener<T> listener)
      Main procedure of the class.
      void stop()
      Stops the external process: In particular the pipe is closed and the process is destroyed.

      • process

        private Process process

        private ReentrantLock lockProcess
        lock for the process-object in order to guarantee synchronous access: If you want to access on process then acquire first the lock!

    • Method Detail

      • launch

        public void launch(String[] command,
                   String initialMessage,
                   PipeListener<T> listener)
            throws Throwable
        Main procedure of the class. Starts the external process, then it goes sleeping until the process has finished its work.
        Throws:
        Throwable

      • getCommunication

        T getCommunication()
        Call this method only after the pipe has been stopped. It is not thread safe!
        Returns:

      • stop

        public void stop()
        Stops the external process: In particular the pipe is closed and the process is destroyed.
